findings.description: Full card PAN and CVV are written to the application log at INFO level, a severe PCI-DSS violation (cardholder data and prohibited CVV in logs).
charge_order never checks whether the order is already 'paid', so a retry or duplicate call charges the customer's card a second time (no idempotency guard).
On successful charge the UPDATE sets status='paid' and tx_id but never sets paid_at, yet monthly_revenue_report filters on paid_at, so every paid order is invisible to the revenue report.
monthly_revenue_report recomputes revenue from raw unit_price*quantity, ignoring the per-line discount and tax that were actually charged, so reported revenue does not match amounts captured.
If the DB execute/commit raises after gateway.charge succeeds, the card is charged but the order is never marked paid and no compensating refund is issued (money taken, no record).
Transaction id is derived from Python's non-cryptographic hash() truncated by %10**10, giving a predictable, collision-prone identifier unsuitable for a payment reference.
compute_order_total applies tax to the subtotal and returns an unrounded float; the unrounded total flows into int(round(total*100)), exposing the charge amount to floating-point drift.
Monetary values use float (unit_price) throughout instead of Decimal, an inherent accuracy hazard for currency arithmetic and accumulation.
discount_pct is never bounds-checked; a value >100 yields a negative discounted line (negative subtotal) and a negative value inflates the charge above list price.
monthly_revenue_report issues one order_items query per order inside the loop (N+1 query pattern) instead of a single aggregate/JOIN.
charge_order performs read-compute-charge-write without any transaction isolation or row lock, so concurrent calls can race on the same order's status.
card_number is never validated for length/digits/Luhn; an empty string yields card_number[-4:]=='' rather than a clear rejection.
A legitimately free order (total rounds to 0, e.g. 100% discount) hits the amount_cents<=0 guard and fails to complete instead of recording a $0 fulfilled order.
charge_order surfaces gateway/DB exceptions raw with no graceful handling or logging beyond the pre-charge INFO line, so failures are not distinguished or recorded.
